publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-08-17 00:05:11 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

origination.label: Creator
origination.persname: Borden, David, 1938- (Title of work: Continuing story of counterpoint..)

origination.label: Creator
origination.persname: Borden, David, 1938- (Name of a part/section of a work: Enfield in winter.. Title of work: Places, times..)

did.abstract: Score sheets, CDs, DVDs, music notebooks with handwritten musical notations, posters of musical compositions with handwritten notes and scores, publicity material, including original and copies of newspaper and magazine reviews and articles, posters, programs, and flyers from concerts and festivals, photographs, and a few personal files of the American minimalistic composer of electronic music, keyboard player, and Cornell University Professor of Music, David Borden (b. 1938), serve to document his composing, performing, and teaching activities dating from 1945-2020.

bioghist.head: Biographical Note
bioghist.p: David Russel Borden (b. 1938, 25 December), an American minimalistic composer of electronic music, keyboard player, and Cornell University Emeritus of Music Department, was born in Boston, Massachusetts. He graduated from Eastman School of Music and Harvard University and was a Fulbright student at the Hochschule für Musik in Berlin. His first composition teachers were Jimmy Giuffre and Jaki Byard. In 1969, he founded the Mother Mallard's Portable Masterpiece Co. (disbanded in 1978), the first all-synthesizer ensemble, with the support of Robert Moog and his Modular Synthesizer systems and MiniMoog instruments. He continued creating and performing solo compositions. In 1981, he created music for Amplified Keyboard Instruments. The twelve-part cycle of pieces for synthesizers, acoustic instruments, and voice, called "The Continuing Story of Counterpoint" was labored for over 11 years and has been called the Goldberg Variations of minimalism. His work is related to the minimalistic style of Philip Glass and Steve Reich. He composed part of the soundtrack for The Exorcist, 1973, and other films. His music is available on the Cuneiform, New World Records, Lameduck and Arbiter labels. He is the founder and retired director of the Digital Music Program (later the Cornell Electroacoustic Music Center). He is married to Rebecca Goldin and lives in Ithaca, NY. He also collaborates and performs with his son, Gabriel Borden and stepson, Sam Godin.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: David Borden papers, #14-20-4107. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The David Borden Papers document the composing, performing, and teaching activities of the American minimalistic composer of electronic music, keyboard player, and Cornell University Professor of Music, David Borden (b. 1938), dating from 1945-2020. The collection primarily includes David Borden's composing work, with various-size score sheets, music notebooks with handwritten musical notations and scores, and posters of musical compositions with handwritten notes and scores. The collection also contains CDs and DVDs with David Borden's musical compositions and concerts; publicity material with original and copies of newspaper and magazine reviews and articles, posters, programs, and flyers from concerts and festivals, photographs of David Borden and collaborators, as well as +a few personal files with letters and postcards from friends, a letter to his mother and a student list from when Borden was a graduate student.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No. 1 Prelude to Angels; No. 6 Afterwards.

did.unittitle: "Angels No. 2 Light; and No. 4 UFO". Copies
"Angels No. 2 Light (Guitar, Ellen); and Pt.1 Angelic Order"

scopecontent.p: By Charles Harrington (Cornell University), By Jon Reis (1995); By Steve J. Sherman (1995), and Peter Schaaf (1991); Photographs of David Borden primarily; Ed Murray, and Ellen Hargis (1994).

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Included copyrighted photographs. See the physical object.

did.unittitle: Reviews for the Continuing Story of Counterpoint Parts 9-12

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies of biographical notes, reviews, and articles. Include relevant correspondence. Relevant material in Box 17, Folder 5. Newspaper articles about Robert Moog.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included newspaper reviews, posters programs and flyers from concerts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: David Borden's name is on the list, as a student of Berkshire Music Center, along with his local and home address and his department (Composition).

scopecontent.p: Published poet. Postcards, handwritten letters.
